summary: Window issues

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducable in 4.3 stable

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable (77dcf97d8) - Windows 10.0.22631 - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.3820) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-14900HX (32 Threads)

### Issue description

Ive integrated some windows into my game for a better ui, and I noticed a bunch of issues:

1. When you add a menu button on a native windows os window, it opens on the main screen and not on the actual window. This doesn not happen when you use godots built in windows
2. Setting the force native propperty on a window, while it being visible in the scene and then starting the game gives an error. I have to work around this by connecting the ready signal to popup
3. Content scale does not work on windows, both built in and os. I tried canvas item and expand, simply nothing happens when I resize the window

Here are some annoyences that I found, and might be worth fixing:

1. You can verry easily move the top bar of a built in window off screen, at least on windows, and then you can't grab it anymore, which results in you being unable to close the window or move it back down. I suggest that if the user double clicks on the side of a window, which would normally resize it, it instead allows the user to drag the window around as if they clicked the top bar of the window
2. In the editor, once you put something in a window, its boarders are not visible anymore. Like the orange marks that show you how big it is. This is really anoying when figuring out positioning
3. This one might be too much, but it would be really cool if godot allowed automatic window resizing like windows does when you drag a window to the top, it give you options to split the screen in half, in three parts and so on (This thing) 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13a4bca0-55e2-4515-a031-77dcb3b07eaa)
4. It would be great if it were possible the hide the close button on built in windows
5. Also resizing the close button would be nice, to make it bigger for mobile users

### Steps to reproduce

Just start the mrp

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Quick notes on the mrp, I added the exact same window twice, once as a built in one and once as an os one. The os one has the ready signal connected to it because of the previously mentioned issue. The menu button has a short script that just adds two test items that do nothing
[window-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18883677/window-mrp.zip)

summary: Expose `ClassDB::is_class_exposed` to scripting

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10216
**Does not close, but allows working around these:** https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/9160 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/7281

This function is useful to any type of dynamic workflow that may want to create scripts during runtime that uses classes from ClassDB.

Exposing it can prevent parsing errors by filtering out unavailable classes.

## Changes
- Added bindings in `core_bind.h` and `core_bind.cpp`
- Added function documentation in `ClassDB.xml`

I'm not well versed in the engine code and in advanced C++, so I copied the implementation of `ClassDB::is_class_enabled`.

## Sample Project
[exposed-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18880532/exposed-test.zip)
This project shows two ways of listing available classes. Use the buttons available in the inspector to test them.

The first is the current and only way, by brute forcing. It uses a dynamic script that runs for every class in ClassDB and identifies the ones that don't throw errors.
It results in 850 parse errors, and it must cache the failing names to not crash during runtime.

The second is using the exposed function to filter out unavailable classes before running the dynamic script.
It results in only 3 errors that only happen due to 3 classes with known problems (see https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/52162 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/86132), and it does not require any caching for runtime.

summary: Unexpected behavior when interacting with another software: Windows Power Toys (Always on Top)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Godot Version 4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]
- Windows power toys v0.88.0

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.22631 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 7 3800XT 8-Core Processor (16 Threads)

### Issue description

Windows Power Toys is software by microsoft for windows which add aditional utility options and customization to user experience to WindowsOS. Always on top is one of the power toys: it can be used to pin windows to the top of the screen, so that even when another window which would cover it up gains focus, it remains visable.

Godot is godot. It has the ability to make some of its editor docks 'float' as seporate windows to the main program. I can the move them about, like to a second monitor.

Using Power Toys: Aot, I can pin these floating windows to the top of my screen. which allows me to spread godot out to multiple screens without risking losing sight of them if I click on another program which is full-screen on that second monitor. This is a useful interaction betwixt softwares, even considering the bug I'm about to describe.

The bug: when the floating windows are pinned as described above, the rename text box and mouse2 menus render behind the pinned window, completely and near completely obscurred respectively, which is not the intended nor expected behavior. This bug also presents when pinning the main window (additionally, when pinning the main window, the scene, project, etc drop menus render behind as well).


### Steps to reproduce

Set up:
- Install Godot 4.3.stable.official.
- Install Windows Power Toys v0.88.0 (newest version at time of writing).
- Set up and activate Always On Top.
- Open Godot, any project.
- Make any editor dock float (In my case I float scene and file systems).
- Pin them and the main window using PT:Aot.

Observe behavior as you:
- Open the scene, project, etc drop down menus
- M2 click to bring up the m2-menu.
- Double click a node or file / press F2 to enter renaming that object.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Add signals `scene_started` and `scene_stopped` to EditorInterface.

description.text:
Adds signals `scene_started` and `scene_stopped` to EditorInterface that are emitted when the EditorInterface has started or stopped playing a scene.

The purpose of this is particularly to allow EditorScripts to be notified when a scene has finished so that it can then continue with other work (such as playing another scene). 

You can find more discussion about this feature in this [forum post](https://forum.godotengine.org/t/how-to-make-multiple-movie-recordings-await-end-of-play/101069) and this [feature request](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/3504). An alternative that might work is busy-waiting on `EditorInterface.is_playing_scene()`, but that seems clearly worse than providing a signal to await.

The implementation works by subscribing the EditorInterface to the EditorRunBar `play_pressed` and `stop_pressed` signals and forwarding them along.

Closes godotengine/godot-proposals#3504

summary: Loading a zip resource pack inside a resource pack fails with "ERROR: Condition "!zfile" is true. Returned: false" after v3.6.beta4

description.text:
### Tested versions

#### 4.x
- Does not work in 4.5 dev 4 (_bug squad edit_)

#### 3.x
- Works in v3.5.3.stable.official.6c814135b
- Works in v3.6.beta1.official.632a544c6
- Works in v3.6.beta2.official.68c507f59
- Works in v3.6.beta3.official.21ab700f2
- Works in v3.6.beta4.official.93ac21cf8
- Does not work in v3.6.beta5.official.2a347ab86
- Does not work in v3.6.stable.official.de2f0f147


### System information

Windows 10


### Issue description

In Godot 3.6 Beta 4 and earlier, it was possible to load a resource pack (e.g. with ProjectSettings.load_resource_pack("...")) that can contain additional resource packs to load. This is used for modding or project organisation (where one resource pack is a complete mod/dlc that can contain additional resources to load at a specific location or with a specific priority / order / logic).

While it is certainly possible to develop a modding / organisation strategy without packs in packs, it breaks all mods and projects that use such a system.



### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP.
2. Run the main scene.
3. The window shows an OK image if everything could be loaded. Nothing if there was a problem (and error messages in the console)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ef5ac322-9d36-4e90-bcf5-758c4bf38378)

Create your own:
A. zip a resource/folder (e.g. a .png)
B. zip the created zip (with other files you need)
C. Load the resources with ProjectSettings.load_resource_pack(). _This loads all resources from step B into the project._
D. Try load the content of the internal zip from step A with ProjectSettings.load_resource_pack(). _This fails in newer versions._
E. (The .png can not be loaded if step D failed)

Error message at step D:
```
Godot Engine v3.6.stable.official.de2f0f147 - https://godotengine.org
OpenGL ES 3.0 Renderer: NVIDIA GeForce
Async. shader compilation: OFF

ERROR: Condition "!zfile" is true. Returned: false
  at: try_open_pack (core/io/file_access_zip.cpp:171) - Condition "!zfile" is true. Returned: false
```



### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[LoadRegression.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18877130/LoadRegression.zip)

I've added variables for almost each step so you can set breakpoints and directly inspect step by step what happens.
The last line shows that the .zip from step A can be loaded directly without problems.

summary: Add UndoRedo actions to preview sun/environment popup

description.text:

First time contributing, so let me know if I screwed up something :)

Wasn't able to figure out how to directly access shortcut events on popups, so I made the popup into an internal class that overrides shortcut_input. Let me know if there's a better way to do this or if you want to move more stuff into it.

summary: Loading patch PCK in game launched from editor results in old resources being instantiated

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.beta [e567f49cbb292dd6bb628348e67c1f3c2a3a5f99]

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta (e567f49cb) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon(TM) Graphics (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.11029.1008) - AMD Ryzen 9 7940HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

When creating a patch PCK that's meant to replace some resource, and then loading said patch in a game session launched from the editor (or when just passing the main scene file to the editor binary) you'll sometimes (always?) find that the game still instantiates the previous version of the resource.

This does not happen when doing the same thing in an exported build.

### Steps to reproduce

(I have included a patch PCK called `patch.pck` that replaces the label text in `main.tscn` to be "Patched" instead of "Not patched".)

1. Open the MRP.
2. Run the main scene.
3. Note how it says "Not patched".
4. Set up an export preset for the platform you're on.
5. Make sure to include `*.pck` under "Filters to export non-resource files/folders".
6. Export a build using said preset.
7. Run the exported build.
8. Note how it says "Patched".

summary: Replace editor renderer dropdown with icon next to version button

description.text:
- Alternative to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/103016.

The rendering driver is represented by the icon, while the rendering method is represented by its color (green for Forward+, purple for Mobile, blue for Compatibility).

This allows saving space at the top of the editor while providing more information than before on editor screenshots and videos, which is useful for technical support purposes. As a bonus, the rendering driver and method are now visible in the project manager.

EditorVersionButton is now based on Button instead of LinkButton so that it can display an icon. However, this means its text is no longer underlined on hover.

In a future PR, we can also expand the usage of these icons to be displayed next to each project on the project list.

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11806.

summary: Don't disable IME on focus out

description.text:
Fixes #89285

When the editor looses focus on Linux to another text field or another window, the editor scrolls to the caret position. It can be very annoying when switching repeatedly in and out of the Godot editor. This behavior was introduced in #70052.

Maybe the intention wasn't disabling IME but to cancel any input in progress. Doing this, the unwanted scrolling doesn't happen and testing with the composition key on Gnome, any in-progress composed input is cancelled.

Could @bruvzg or @raulsntos confirm this?

summary: Scripts in @tool mode can assign a resource of invalid type when that resource is nested inside another resource

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproductible in **v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]** and **v4.4.beta4.official [93d270693]** - no error is shown, incorrect resource becomes null at runtime but remains unchanged in scene.
- In **v4.2.2.stable.official [15073afe3]** and **v4.1.4.stable.official [fe0e8e557]** the resource can be assigned incorrectly as well, but reloading inspector results in error message "Failed to set a resource of the type 'ResourceA (Resource)' because this EditorResourcePicker only accepts 'ResourceB' and its derivatives." 
When looking at scene file as text, `resource_b` still references `resource_a`
```
[ext_resource type="Resource" uid="uid://dbk7c3j5r1xj1" path="res://resources/resource_a.tres" id="2_0b8se"]
[ext_resource type="Resource" uid="uid://btfqm51nwl7t" path="res://resources/resource_b.tres" id="3_3gvcg"]

resource_a = ExtResource("2_0b8se")
resource_b = ExtResource("2_0b8se")
```

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.19045 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7242) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600X 6-Core Processor (12 Threads)

### Issue description

Suppose we have a `ResourceCollection` that contains two resources of different types:
```
class_name ResourceCollection 
extends Resource

@export var resource_a: ResourceA
@export var resource_b: ResourceB
```

We then use `ResourceCollection` in a scene:
```
class_name MainNode
extends Node

@export var resources: ResourceCollection
@export var resource_b: ResourceB


func _ready() -> void:
	print(resources.resource_a)
	print(resources.resource_b)
	
	if resources.resource_a:
		print(resources.resource_a.some_variable) # some_variable = 12
	if resources.resource_b:
		print(resources.resource_b.some_other_variable) # some_other_variable = "Hello!"
```
Running the scene prints the expected output:
```
<Resource#-9223372008383838961>
<Resource#-9223372008216066798>
12
Hello!
```
Assigning correct resources (`resource_a` and `resource_b`) in Inspector works as intended. However, the problem appears when we try to use a script in `@tool` mode to assign such nested resources. I'm using a custom dock plugin with one button that attempts to assign `resource_a` to variable of type `ResourceB`:
```
func _on_button_pressed() -> void:
	print("Before:", edited_node.resources.resource_b)
	# Assigning ResourceA to variable of type ResourceB
	edited_node.resources.resource_b = load("res://resources/resource_a.tres")
	print("After:", edited_node.resources.resource_b)
	
	# This also happens when resources are exported directly
	edited_node.resource_b = load("res://resources/resource_a.tres")
```

**The expected outcome is that it shows an error and prevents any changes.** 
Instead, the resource is indeed changed:
```
Before:<Resource#-9223370309707436114>
After:<Resource#-9223370309891985493>
```
Note that these two resources identifiers are not the same. Furthermore, when taking a look at the inspector, there's clearly `resource_a` assigned to a variable of type `ResourceB`:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/44a7cf55-1e2f-49d9-8bb1-5a61fd8e6bff)

Running the scene in this state results in the invalid resource being null at runtime, but it remains unchanged after stopping the game:
```
<Resource#-9223372008383838961>
<null>
12
```

If we take a look at the scene file in text format, we can see that `resource_a` is referenced in both variables of `ResourceCollection`:
```
[ext_resource type="Resource" uid="uid://dbk7c3j5r1xj1" path="res://resources/resource_a.tres" id="2_0b8se"]
[ext_resource type="Script" path="res://resource_collection/resource_collection.gd" id="2_bwhhm"]
[ext_resource type="Resource" uid="uid://btfqm51nwl7t" path="res://resources/resource_b.tres" id="3_3gvcg"]

[sub_resource type="Resource" id="Resource_nybud"]
script = ExtResource("2_bwhhm")
resource_a = ExtResource("2_0b8se")
resource_b = ExtResource("2_0b8se")
```

### Steps to reproduce

The MRP contains a "main scene" which is a plain Node exporting `ResourceCollection` and a sample dock plugin with a button that on press, attempts to assign `resource_a` to a variable of type `ResourceB` inside `ResourceCollection`.
1. Check the main scene to see that you can only assign resources of type `ResourceB` to the `resource_b` exported property.
2. Enable the plugin (if not enabled already)
3. Switch to newly added dock and select `MainNode` in the main scene
4. Click the `Assign Invalid Resource B` button (note: the button is not visible until you select a single valid node)
5. Check the exported resources in the Inspector. It should display `resource_a` in both cases. Run the game to confirm the second resource is null at  runtime.

summary: "Bone Angle" does not show up in UV editor

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.3.stable.flathub [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable (77dcf97d8) - Freedesktop SDK 24.08 (Flatpak runtime) - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon Graphics (RADV RENOIR) - AMD Ryzen 7 PRO 4750U with Radeon Graphics (16 Threads)

### Issue description

When using the *Bone Angle* property to rotate a bone, it does not show up in the UV editor. Is that intended?

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a polygon, a skeleton and a bone as a child of the skeleton.
2. Add a texture to the polygon.
3. Change the *Length* and *Bone Angle* property of the bone.
4. Attach the skeleton to the polygon.
5. Open the UV editor of the polygon, click "Bones", then "Sync Bones to Polygon"
6. Observe that the *Bone Angle* is not applied to the shown skeleton.

summary: Reflection probes cause massive CPU spike when using Unshaded view in the Compatibility Renderer

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5 stable
- Reproducible in v4.4.beta4.official [93d270693] 
- Reproducible in 4.3 stable
- Not reproducible in 4.2 stable, because Reflection Probes did not yet work using the Compatibility renderer

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta4 - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 SUPER (nvidia; 550.120) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

As soon as at least two Reflection Probes overlap in the Compatibility renderer while the viewport is set to unshaded, a massive CPU spike occurs and the editor can even become fully unresponsive . The problem only affects the Compatibility renderer. It doesn't get worse with more than two overlapping Reflection Probes, but is does get worse with every additional mesh that is affected by the probes.

Watch the CPU time in the following video and notice how the editor starts to lag heavily.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e00fe5e8-7290-4fb8-aba5-2beaa7db4027

### Steps to reproduce

- Open the MRP
- Show frame time (Perspective -> View Frame Time)
- Switch to unshaded view (Perspective -> View Unshaded)
- Select the second Reflection Probe and drag it over the cubes
- Observe CPU Time

summary: Always allow selecting any rendering driver in the settings, add "auto" option.

description.text:
See https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/103006#issuecomment-2666838682

- All rendering driver names can be selected in the project setting (for both compatibility and rendering device), regardless of the editor build config.
- "auto" option is added (and set as default for some platforms) to allow auto selection of the first available driver (in the following order: `metal`, `vulkan`, `d3d12`).

summary: Skeleton's deferred update process should be performed after physics interpolation

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproduced in v4.4.beta4.official [93d270693]

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta4 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-12900K (24 threads)

### Issue description

Enabling physics interpolation in project settings and following procedures to manually implement interpolation for camera movement ([trying to follow this](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/3.6/tutorials/physics/interpolation/advanced_physics_interpolation.html#mouse-look)) results in the SkeletonIK3D failing to have physics interpolation while moving and looking around, while everything else is smooth:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0645a0ac-7f8b-48fe-a912-1e8695df23ec


It is noteworthy that disabling physics interpolation globally but keeping the manual implementation on the camera results in smooth ik movement when looking around but NOT while walking:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bf066442-43d8-4549-9d8b-55cc49cd6035

It's entirely possible I am implementing this incorrectly, in which case, better documentation/information would be really helpful, as currently it seems unclear how to properly implement first person camera mouse movement with physics interpolation on high refresh monitors and inverse kinematics.

### Steps to reproduce

summary: TileSet Polygon editor doesn't use all available area

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.beta4.mono.official [93d270693]

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta4.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-3570K CPU @ 3.40GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

The polygon editor used for editing tileset's physics polygons does not use all the available vertical space. The polygon editor height stays at 200 pixels, which is set to its minimum size. Using the polygon editor feels quite cramped because of this.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8ca2f4d5-0b63-4b58-8866-b4b592f92a76)

I gave the code a quick look and it seems that the intent of the original author was to make the polygon editor fill all vertical space: 

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/e948f52ea93cfff23b4a24b83d310724be2fb208/editor/plugins/tiles/tile_data_editors.cpp#L986

But for some reason it doesn't work. I tried to sprinkle that `set_v_size_flags(Control::SIZE_EXPAND_FILL)` to several other places, but without any success.

Occlusion layer's polygon editor has the same problem and there may be other similar cases elsewhere.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add `TileMapLayer`
2. Add `TileSet` to the `TileMapLayer`
3. Add texture to the `TileSet` 
4. Add physics layer to the `TileSet` 
5. Go to the tileset editor, Paint | Select a property editor | Physics Layer 0
6. Resize bottom panel and notice that the polygon editor area doesn't expand vertically

summary: Loading patch PCK on Android with big project is slow

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.beta [93d270693079ea7802c9e1334a2e0ecd8529eeed]

### System information

OnePlus 8T, Android 14

### Issue description

When loading patch PCKs on Android currently, it seems to take a long time to load the first patch when you have many files in the project, which upon investigation seems to stem from the changes made in #90425.

When profiling the MRP (consisting of 5000 barebones scene files) through Android Studio it would seem that it spends about 30 seconds (on my OnePlus 8T) in `PackedSourceDirectory::try_open_pack`, which is what gets run ([here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/e948f52ea93cfff23b4a24b83d310724be2fb208/core/config/project_settings.cpp#L493)) when you first load a non-main pack.

More specifically, it seems to spend a lot of time dealing with `android::ZipAssetsProvider` when doing things like `DirAccessJAndroid::dir_exists` as part of `DirAccess::open` and `DirAccessJAndroid::current_is_dir` as part of `DirAccess::get_directories`.

Here's what the flame graph of the MRP's startup looks like:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0f947ca7-de59-4e20-a9fb-ba1b181e54e1)

Here's a bottom-up view:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4c2e4dc5-ae25-47b1-80ce-977f94262ad6)

Here's a zoomed in view of a few `DirAccess::get_directories` calls:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8c4c01d1-08c1-4e5a-8304-f20641fdd84c)

Here's a zoomed in view of a few `DirAccess::open` calls:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2f2028fc-6448-4e8f-819d-d294bba838d9)

### Steps to reproduce

I've included a patch PCK in the project, under `patches/patch.pck`. All it does is change the label in `main.tscn` to say "Patched" instead of "Not patched".

I've also included the script (`generate.py`) used to generate the scene files under `generated_scenes`.

summary: Instanced Control with anchor preset of Top Left becomes centered on axis when anchor preset of instance is modified and position matches anchor position

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.0.4, 4.1.4, 4.2.2, 4.3 stable, and 4.4 beta 4

### System information

Arch Linux

### Issue description

When a Control node is the root of a scene and has its anchor preset set to Top Left and it is instantiated in another Control node with its anchor preset overridden to Center Top or Center Right (may happen with other centers) and its X or Y position is the same as the center position, it will be fully centered on the anchors on that axis, rather than being where it was placed (having its axis meet the anchor X or Y position).

This also affects the saving of the scene, saving the scene and running it will cause the saved scene to have the Controls position centered on the axis which meets the anchor.

When loading the MRP, you may need to manually change the child Control's position to be centered as it doesn't save properly.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38808777-95e0-4d4a-a634-7635dd9f4def)

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a Control scene with anchor preset Full Rect.
- Instantiate or create a new Control scene with anchor preset Center Top.
- Ensure the child scene has the anchor preset Top Left.
- Move the instantiated child scene so that its X position is at the center of the root Control (576 with the default 1152x648).
- Run the root control scene.

summary: TextureRect does not follow CanvasItem texture repeat settings, which means mirrored repeat can't be used

description.text:
- *Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/51625.*

### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3.stable, 4.4.beta3

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta (93d270693) - Fedora Linux 41 (KDE Plasma) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (nvidia; 565.77) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900K (32 threads)

### Issue description

TextureRect does not follow CanvasItem texture repeat settings, which means mirrored repeat can't be used:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/156d41b1-7a49-4df3-997f-2552e2185842)

In contrast, it works with Sprite2D when **Region** is enabled. This occurs in all rendering methods.

This is likely because TextureRect implements repeat using `draw_texture_rect()` instead of `draw_rect_region()` like Sprite2D does.

Sprite2D drawing code: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/93d270693079ea7802c9e1334a2e0ecd8529eeed/scene/2d/sprite_2d.cpp#L118-L130

TextureRect drawing code: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/93d270693079ea7802c9e1334a2e0ecd8529eeed/scene/gui/texture_rect.cpp#L98


### Steps to reproduce

- Add a TextureRect node with its scale mode set to **Tile**, and give it a texture.
- Resize the TextureRect so the texture is displayed multiple times.
- Set the texture repeat mode to **Mirrorred** on the TextureRect node.

summary: Asset Library prints "TLS handshake error: -9984" when a given asset's icon uses an invalid TLS certificate

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.beta4 (93d270693079ea7802c9e1334a2e0ecd8529eeed)
- Reproducible in 4.3.stable
- Didn't test earlier versions but likely reproducible too, it's not a bug per se, just too verbose an error log.

### System information

Fedora Linux 41 (KDE Plasma) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7600M XT (RADV NAVI33) - AMD Ryzen 7 8845HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

When browsing the asset library, some assets may have an icon (possibly also screenshot when viewing the asset details) hosted on a domain with an invalid TLS certificate. Godot thus rightfully refuses to load such icon, and prints this error in the output:

In 4.3.stable:
```
thirdparty/mbedtls/library/ssl_tls.c:8006: x509_verify_cert() returned -9984 (-0x2700)
ERROR: TLS handshake error: -9984
   at: _do_handshake (modules/mbedtls/stream_peer_mbedtls.cpp:89)
mbedtls error: returned -0x2700
```

In 4.4.beta4:
```
ERROR: TLS handshake error: -9984
   at: _do_handshake (modules/mbedtls/stream_peer_mbedtls.cpp:88)
mbedtls error: returned -0x2700
```

This is reproduced for example with https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/2995 in the Project Manager, or https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/2458 in a Godot project.

The error means invalid certificate so it's correct, but it's unnecessary information to provide to Godot users who just browse the asset library. This error should likely be made verbose only, or we might want to outright silence TLS handshake errors in the asset library, as the surface for such errors is high since we source icons and screenshots externally.

For the new Asset Store, we should make sure that the backend validates URLs that it would expose to clients so that we don't have such issues (CC @coppolaemilio @winston-yallow).

### Steps to reproduce

- Open Asset Library in Project Manager
- Search for "XR Fragments" https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/2995
- Check terminal output

Or:

- Open Asset Library in a Godot project
- Search for "2090s Language System" https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/2458
- Check Output dock

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

n/a

description.text:

v4.4.beta3.official [06acfccf8]

### Issue description

When using `LightmapGI` with `LightmapProbes` object set to GI mode `Dynamic` (GeometryInstance3D/Global Illumination/Mode) are too bright in dark areas and don't receive the baked lighting properly. This is the case with both manually placed and automatic probes. Sometimes things look just like with GI disabled:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/eadeee86-d5e4-4899-987a-97681bda1846)

The left box is set to static the right one dynamic. The right box should look dark and orange tinted like the left one.

This might be the same bug as #69098 that survived the cpu -> gpu move.

### Steps to reproduce

- Build a dark room with two mesh instances inside
- Set one mesh to static GI and the other to dynamic
- Add a `LightmapGI` and a `LightmapProbe` in the room
- Bake your lightmap
- The dynamic mesh will be brighter than the static one

summary: Favorite properties aren't stored across projects

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4 beta 3

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6117) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

The new favorite property feature invaluable for speeding up common operations in the inspector but it is quite frustrating that you have to repeat the process of assigning favorites for every single project.

I don't know where the data for favorite properties is stored. My naive proposal is to allow storing favorite properties as editor settings. Maybe add a section to the editor settings where you can enter the node type and the property path like below so that the properties will be favorites across projects. 

```
- Node2D:position
- Control:custom_minimum_size
- Control:mouse_filter
```

This approach could work for custom types, too. If the properties or the class isn't found, we can just skip it.

```
- Enemy:base_health
- ToggleArea2D:is_disabled
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Click `Favorite Property` on any property in one project
2. Open another project and you will notice that the property is not a favorite
